task: Fix `.env` loading in `askr init` — friend's cloned repo not reading Discord webhook URL from copied `.env.example`
discussion_summary: Root cause identified: `~/.config/askr/.env` (created on first run with just API key) was being loaded and returning early, preventing the repo's `.env` from ever being read. Previous session pushed a fix to `env.py`, but the friend needed to `git pull` it. This session verified the fix and then discovered a second issue: `env.load()` call in `askr.py` was wrapped in a bare `except Exception: pass` that hid the actual error. Solution: load `.env` directly from `ASKR_DIR` in `cmd_init()` using `dotenv.load_dotenv()` with explicit path, surfacing any errors instead of silencing them.

accomplishments.what: Identified that `.env.example` exists in repo and friend correctly copied it with own keys
Confirmed previous `env.py` fix was already committed and friend had pulled it
Modified `/Users/bippin/Desktop/askr/askr/cli/askr.py` to load `.env` directly from `ASKR_DIR` in `cmd_init()` instead of relying on global `env.load()`
Committed fix: 'fix: load .env from ASKR_DIR directly in cmd_init'

in_progress.what: Modified `cmd_init()` to call `dotenv.load_dotenv(ASKR_DIR / '.env')` directly with error handling instead of relying on module-level `env.load()` which was being shadowed by `~/.config/askr/.env`

next_actions.action: Friend runs `git pull` in cloned repo to get the latest `askr.py` fix
next_actions.why: The fix was just committed; friend needs the updated code to test

next_actions.action: Friend runs `askr init` again and verify that the Discord webhook URL from `.env` is now correctly registered
next_actions.why: This is the actual test case — webhook should be picked up from repo `.env` on init

next_actions.action: If webhook still not registering, check `~/.config/askr/.env` to see what was written there and confirm it's being overridden by repo `.env` load
next_actions.why: The fix loads repo `.env` after `ASKR_DIR/.env` is set up, so repo values should take precedence

next_actions.action: Verify that error messages (if any) are now visible instead of silently caught by `except Exception: pass`
next_actions.why: Surfacing errors will help diagnose any remaining issues instead of silent failures

decisions.decision: Load `.env` directly in `cmd_init()` using `dotenv.load_dotenv(ASKR_DIR / '.env')` instead of relying on module-level `env.load()`
decisions.reason: Module-level `env.load()` was being shadowed by `~/.config/askr/.env` existing from a previous run, preventing repo `.env` from ever being read. Direct load in `cmd_init()` ensures repo `.env` is loaded at the right time with explicit path.

decisions.decision: Remove bare `except Exception: pass` and surface actual errors
decisions.reason: Silent exception handling was hiding the real problem; explicit error handling lets us see what's actually failing

failed_approaches.approach: Relying on global `env.load()` in `env.py` to handle both `~/.config/askr/.env` and repo `.env`
failed_approaches.reason: The function was returning early after loading `~/.config/askr/.env`, never reaching the repo `.env` load. Order of operations and early return made this approach fragile.

failed_approaches.approach: Wrapping `env.load()` call in bare `except Exception: pass`
failed_approaches.reason: Silenced errors and made debugging impossible; actual failures were hidden from the user

relational_files.why: Previous session's fix to this file was already committed; this session's fix in `askr.py` bypasses the problematic `env.load()` call
Friend copied this to `.env` with own keys; the fix ensures this file's values are actually loaded during `askr init`
Confirms `.env` is gitignored, so repo `.env` only exists on machines where it was manually created
